message.ecclientsettings.action_failed: The client action could not be saved; the previous setting is still active
message.ecclientsettings.hud_editor.save: HUD save failed; your changes remain in the editor.
message.ecclientsettings.profile.recover: %s Profile recovery warning(s) were recorded; see the log for preserved file paths
message.ecclientsettings.profile.stale_d: The active Profile changed; reopen settings before saving
message.ecclientsettings.settings.save_f: Settings could not be saved. Your draft has been kept.
Changes only local lightmap inputs; it never changes the saved brightness option or potion effects.
option.ecclientsettings.fullbright.stren: Blends from vanilla at 0.0 to the selected local brightness effect at 1.0.
option.ecclientsettings.block_outline.co: Sets the outline's alpha and RGB color without changing its shape, width, reach, or depth behavior.
option.ecclientsettings.block_outline.en: Recolors the vanilla outline of the block you are targeting.
option.ecclientsettings.low_fire.enabled: Moves only the vanilla first-person fire overlay downward.
Recolors only the existing vanilla hurt overlay, including its transparency.
option.ecclientsettings.hit_color.enable: Uses vanilla hurt timing, visibility, and depth rules for every visible hurt entity.
option.ecclientsettings.hud.armor.enable: Shows equipped items and their durability.
option.ecclientsettings.hud.description: HUD settings belong to the current Profile. Right Shift opens all client settings; use the visual editor here to move, scale, and style widgets.
Open editor (drag / scroll scale / edit style)
Shows the client's current frames per second.
option.ecclientsettings.hud.keystrokes.e: Shows movement, jump, and mouse-button press states with optional CPS.
option.ecclientsettings.hud.left_cps.ena: Shows left-click CPS as an independent HUD widget with its own position, scale, and style.
option.ecclientsettings.hud.ping.enabled: Shows connection latency, or -- when no valid value is available.
option.ecclientsettings.hud.potions.enab: Shows active potion effects, amplifiers, and remaining time.
option.ecclientsettings.hud.right_cps.en: Shows right-click CPS as an independent HUD widget with its own position, scale, and style.
option.ecclientsettings.hud_style.animat: 0.1-2.0 color cycles or switches per second; capped for readability and to avoid rapid flashing.
option.ecclientsettings.hud_style.descri: These options affect only the selected HUD widget. Choose a text mode first, then tune its color and low-frequency RGB animation.
option.ecclientsettings.hud_style.paddin: GUI pixels between widget content and its panel edge.
option.ecclientsettings.hud_style.previe: Fix invalid values to resume preview
option.ecclientsettings.hud_style.rainbo: Controls the hue difference between adjacent characters; used only by Rainbow Wave.
option.ecclientsettings.hud_style.text_c: Fixed mode uses the complete color; RGB modes use its alpha channel.
option.ecclientsettings.hud_style.text_m: Fixed is easiest to read; Rainbow Wave moves across characters; Switch changes the whole line at a low frequency.
option.ecclientsettings.keystrokes.descr: Choose which actions to show and style idle and pressed states independently. The outer panel and idle text color/RGB are in the HUD Style category.
option.ecclientsettings.keystrokes.press: 0 switches immediately; 80-140 ms is recommended for readable clicks without a trailing feel.
Pressed keys use this fixed accent; idle keys use the fixed or RGB mode from HUD Style.
option.ecclientsettings.keystrokes.show_: Counts physical left/right presses in the last second without consuming or changing game input.
option.ecclientsettings.time_changer.cus: Visible day-cycle time from 0 to 23999, used by Custom mode.
option.ecclientsettings.time_changer.mod: Changes only the local sky time and keeps tracking server time for accurate restoration.
option.ecclientsettings.zoom.animation_s: Controls smooth transition duration from 1 (slow) to 10 (fast).
option.ecclientsettings.zoom.divisor.too: Base FOV divisor from 1 to 16; larger values zoom in farther.
option.ecclientsettings.zoom.enabled.too: Enables the rebindable Zoom action without changing saved camera options.
option.ecclientsettings.zoom.max_divisor: Upper zoom limit for scroll adjustment, from the base divisor to 32.
option.ecclientsettings.zoom.reduce_sens: Temporarily scales turn sensitivity through NeoForge's turn event.
option.ecclientsettings.zoom.scroll_adju: Consumes vertical scrolling only while Zoom is active and the divisor changes.
option.ecclientsettings.zoom.smooth_came: Temporarily enables cinematic turn smoothing without changing the saved option.
option.ecclientsettings.force_sprint.too: Automatically attempts to sprint while moving forward and still follows vanilla sprint restrictions.
option.ecclientsettings.profile.name: Profile name
option.ecclientsettings.sword_blocking_a: Plays the legacy blocking animation when using a sword. Disabled by default; offhand blocks remain placeable when enabled.
option.ecclientsettings.server_window_fr: Allows the current server to request dark mode or colors for the native title bar. Unsupported platforms safely ignore it.
option.ecclientsettings.server_window_ti: Allows the current server to override the title shown by the game window and taskbar.
Delete Profile '%s'? Its file is preserved as a recovery copy.
screen.ecclientsettings.profiles.title: Profile Management
screen.ecclientsettings.hud_editor.instr: Drag: move / Scroll or +/-: scale / Click: select
Arrows: nudge / Shift: 10 px / Alt: bypass snapping
screen.ecclientsettings.hud_editor.title: Edit HUD Layout
screen.ecclientsettings.hud_style.title: %s Style
screen.ecclientsettings.title: EaseCation Client Settings
option.ecclientsettings.obs_overlay.desc: These are global streaming privacy settings and never change with a PvP Profile. Selected content is hidden from OBS Game Capture while remaining visible in your own game window.
option.ecclientsettings.obs_overlay.safe: Important: only OBS Game Capture is supported. Display Capture, Window Capture, Discord screen sharing, Game Bar, and similar capture methods are not protected. Complete the verification below before every stream.
option.ecclientsettings.obs_overlay.stat: Local protection hook ready (OBS verification still required)
option.ecclientsettings.obs_overlay.save: Saved configuration: master %s / test marker %s (page changes apply only after Done)
option.ecclientsettings.obs_overlay.conf: The local hook may be ready, but the saved master switch is off; no selected content is currently hidden.
option.ecclientsettings.obs_overlay.mark: The master switch is on, but the test marker is off; selected real content still requires verification before streaming.
option.ecclientsettings.obs_overlay.unsa: OBS attached before this mod installed its capture hook. Fully exit OBS and Minecraft, launch Minecraft and enter a world first, then launch OBS. Do not stream until the test passes.
option.ecclientsettings.obs_overlay.fail: Stop streaming immediately. Fully exit OBS and Minecraft, launch Minecraft and enter a world first, then launch OBS and verify again. If it still fails, turn off the master switch and report the issue with latest.log.
option.ecclientsettings.obs_overlay.iris: This Iris version cannot expose shadow-pass state, so experimental in-world protection has been disabled by the safety policy. HUD and Screen paths are unaffected by this warning.

An active Iris shader pack was detected. Experimental in-world protection is disabled and selected elements are hidden locally by fail-closed policy to prevent a bypass. HUD and Screen protection are unaffected.

Danger: an Iris shader pack is active while fail-closed is off, so selected in-world elements cannot be guaranteed hidden from OBS. Do not stream, or disable the shader pack and verify again. HUD and Screen paths are unaffected.
option.ecclientsettings.obs_overlay.comp: Compatibility notice: READY only means the local hook is available; it does not prove the current OBS scene is safe. Third-party HUDs are not protected automatically. Reverify selected real content after installing, updating, or switching render mods, shader packs, capture sources, scenes, fullscreen mode, or resolution.
option.ecclientsettings.obs_overlay.setu: 1. Fully exit OBS. Launch Minecraft first and enter a world; select protected items, enable the master switch and test marker, then click Done to save and return to the game. Confirm the marker is visible locally.
2. Launch OBS afterward. Use only Game Capture for Minecraft; hide or remove every Display/Window Capture source that can see the game.
3. Disable Capture third-party overlays in the Game Capture properties.

4. In both OBS Preview and Program output, verify that the marker and every selected real element are absent while remaining visible locally. Keep the marker on when practical and repeat after any environment change.
option.ecclientsettings.obs_overlay.grou: Auto-hide while a screen is open
option.ecclientsettings.obs_overlay.enab: Enables global streaming privacy protection. Disabled by default; read and complete the verification steps at the top first.
option.ecclientsettings.obs_overlay.show: Continuously verifies the capture pipeline: the marker must be visible locally and absent from OBS Preview and Program output. Keep it enabled while streaming when practical; it does not replace testing real HUD, Screen, and world elements.
option.ecclientsettings.obs_overlay.fail: Hide locally when protection is unavailable
Recommended. If the hook fails or OBS launch order is unsafe, selected content is hidden locally too rather than risk leaking it to the stream.
option.ecclientsettings.obs_overlay.comp: Hides coordinates, facing direction, hardware details, and world information in the F3 debug menu. Protected by default.
Hides chat messages already shown on the HUD.
Hides the chat input field and text currently being typed.
Hides the Tab player list, player names, and server information.
Hides sound subtitles and their direction indicators.
Hides action bar messages above the hotbar.
Hides large titles and subtitles in the center of the screen.
Hides potion and status effect icons on the HUD.
Hides the hotbar, health, armor, hunger, air, experience, item names, and related vanilla HUD elements.
Hides this mod's FPS, ping, CPS, keystrokes, and other custom HUD widgets.
option.ecclientsettings.obs_overlay.worl: Experimental: in-world elements interact with depth buffers and render batches. With Sodium / Iris, verify both without and with a shader pack in the game window and OBS preview.
option.ecclientsettings.obs_overlay.comp: Hides normally visible name tags above entities. ViaBedrockUtility defers names as one batch, so either name option conservatively hides both types in a Bedrock session.
Hides translucent name tags rendered for sneaking entities. ViaBedrockUtility defers names as one batch, so either name option conservatively hides both types in a Bedrock session.
Hides text on signs and hanging signs in the world.
Hides chest-related block entity rendering in the world.
Hides maps in item frames; this does not hide maps held by the player.
Hides banner pattern rendering in the world.
Hides beacon beams in the world.
option.ecclientsettings.obs_overlay.scre: Selected screens are hidden from OBS capture. The marker only checks capture order, not Screen redirection. Open every selected screen before streaming and verify it locally, in OBS Preview, and in Program output.
While a world is loaded, hides any open Screen, including screens added by other mods.
Hides the player's survival inventory screen.
Hides the creative inventory and its search contents.
Hides the command block editor and commands entered in it.
option.ecclientsettings.obs_overlay.cust: Hide custom containers by menu ID
Protects vanilla or modded container screens by menu type ID. This applies only to container-based screens.
One full ID (for example minecraft:generic_9x3) or path (generic_9x3) per row. Find menu IDs in logs or mod documentation.
Invalid menu ID; use namespace:path or a valid path.
option.ecclientsettings.obs_overlay.auto: These options temporarily hide matching HUD / world elements while a non-chat screen is open so they do not cover menus. They do not expand OBS protection.
Hide while a screen is open: %s
Temporarily hides this element locally and from capture while a non-chat screen is open.
option.ecclientsettings.obs_overlay.attr: OBS Overlay technology ported from zziger (Artem Dzhemesiuk).
Opening Profile Management leaves this page and discards HUD, OBS privacy, and other changes that have not been saved with Done. Return and save first is recommended.
